comment.body: chart of market internals on Nasdaq you can see out the gate the TRINQ went from 0.7 to over 2. The moves on the NYSE are not as dramatic...

comment.body: Yep. I pointed this out a couple weeks ago. Saying European automakers sell high end brands in the US market, and China would not be able to fill that void. Moreover, China is dominating bringing cheap, economic models to developing countries (and developed ones if EU was stupid enough to allow it). If European or American automakers want to compete on the global stage, they need to offer dramatically more cost efficient vehicles. China already has its tentacles in SE asia, Africa, etc.
